markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: MLB: Strikeouts Leader - Pitcher
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Will Drake feature Lil Durk on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artists who feature on Drake's album "ICEMAN".

To qualify as "featured", the listed artist must be credited on at least one song on the album according to at least one major streaming platform: namely Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, or YouTube Music.

If the album fails to release by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source of this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 78.5% implied probability for an Iranian agent—defined as someone formally charged with acting at the direction of the Iranian government—being indicted by May 31, reflecting the absence of fresh DOJ actions since the April 20 federal complaint against Shamim Mafi for conspiring to broker arms sales on behalf of Iran's government and IRGC, including 62 contacts with an Iranian intelligence officer. Subsequent developments, like the April 24 unsealing of migrant smuggling charges against Jafar Tafakori, lack allegations of government direction. With three weeks remaining and no announced investigations or hearings, the sporadic pace of prior cases, such as the March conviction of Asif Merchant in a murder-for-hire plot, underpins skepticism for another timely indictment amid ongoing U.S. sanctions enforcement.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This outcome aligns with an exceptionally warm April featuring abundant sunshine and earlier peaks of 30.2°C, consistent with climatological norms for late April (average maxima around 26–27°C) and seasonal forecasts of above-normal temperatures amid a projected record-hot year. markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.5%-3.75% after the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, underscoring solid economic expansion, robust job gains with March unemployment at 4.3%, and persistent inflation as March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year. Trader consensus, reflected in CME FedWatch Tool probabilities and elevated two-year Treasury yields around 3.9%, prices prolonged steady rates through 2026 amid sticky price pressures and resilient labor markets, diminishing near-term cut expectations. Upcoming catalysts include April CPI data on May 12—due before the next FOMC on June 16-17—which could recalibrate market-implied rate paths toward 2027.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 58-59°F on May 2?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Los Angeles International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/los-angeles/KLAX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 44% implied probability to a high temperature of 68-69°F at Los Angeles International Airport on May 2, driven by the National Weather Service's latest guidance forecasting persistent marine layer stratus clouds and patchy morning fog under light onshore winds of 5-10 mph, which promote a low-level temperature inversion capping daytime heating near coastal sites. Cool sea surface temperatures around 60°F exacerbate this cooling influence, aligning with GFS and ECMWF ensemble model runs showing weak high pressure and moisture advection limiting warming to the upper 60s—consistent with May climatology where LAX highs average 68-70°F. Lower odds for 70°F+ reflect low clearing potential, though evening NWS forecast discussions and 00Z model updates could refine trajectories amid inherent short-term forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 500-519 tweets from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against Weibo Gaming, fueled by a 5-3 series record and perfect 2-0 head-to-head sweep this split—including a 2-1 win on April 25—positioning them 4th for direct playoff bracket qualification. AL rides a three-win streak highlighted by a 2-0 sweep over Invictus Gaming on April 28, with jungler Tarzan and top laner Flandre driving macro execution amid Fearless Draft constraints. Weibo Gaming, mired at 3-6 overall despite a recent 2-0 upset over Top Esports, leans on mid laner Xiaohu's experience and Jiejie's ganks but struggles with consistency; no injuries or roster shifts reported, though WBG fights to avoid play-in risks in the double round-robin.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (HIGH) $150 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit Week of April 27 2026?
events.description: What will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit Week of April 27 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Airbnb (ABNB) shares traded in a $136–$144 range during the week of April 27, 2026, stabilizing near $140 after touching a 52-week high of $147 earlier in April, as traders weighed positioning ahead of Q1 2026 earnings on May 7. UBS raised its price target to $153 from $149 on April 27, bolstering sentiment from Q4 2025 results that showed 12% revenue growth to exceed guidance and 16% gross booking value expansion, with full-year 2026 outlook for low double-digit revenue increases. Consensus analyst estimates target $2.62 billion Q1 revenue and $0.30 EPS, amid focus on host supply dynamics, international expansion, and premium listing demand in a resilient travel sector. Volatility may rise post-earnings, with key resistance at $147 and support near $131.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's early April 2026 threats to withdraw the US from NATO, citing European allies' refusal to join US-Israel military actions against Iran, prompted White House spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt to confirm active discussions, spiking short-term speculation. However, Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act prohibits unilateral presidential termination of membership, mandating congressional approval amid strong bipartisan support for the alliance. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte dismissed exit fears on April 19, while signals of potential US troop pullbacks from Germany, Italy, and Spain persist without formal moves. No major developments have occurred in the last 48 hours; traders eye congressional dynamics and alliance summits as key catalysts through 2027.

eventMetadata.context_description: Min Hyung-bae's commanding trader consensus stems from the Democratic Party of Korea's longstanding dominance in the Honam region's local elections, where it routinely secures supermajorities, positioning him as the near-lock for the June 3 inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayor. He clinched the party's nomination on April 14 after a hard-fought primary runoff against incumbent Jeonnam Governor Kim Yung-rok, buoyed by consistent polling leads in pre-runoff surveys showing him ahead by 10-13 points among party voters. People Power Party's Lee Jung-hyun, nominated April 22, trails at minimal odds despite pledging a "30% election revolution" to challenge the monopoly; historical precedents show conservative candidates garnering under 20% in similar races. Recent minor controversy over primary ARS outages was dismissed by the party. Upsets would require a major scandal, health issue for Min, or national wave shifting turnout dramatically in the final month.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Amsterdam be 18°C on May 2?

markets.question: Will Iván Cepeda Castro place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. An Invamer survey (April 15-24) showed Cepeda at 44.3%, de la Espriella at 21.5%, and Valencia at 19.8% after nearly doubling from February via her March primaries win and vice-presidential pick Juan Daniel Oviedo, who polls at 8.2% independently. markets.question: Will ByteDance have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

events.title: Which company has the #1 AI model end of May? (Style Control On)
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic commands an 82% implied probability on Polymarket as traders anticipate Claude Opus 4.7 Thinking will retain the #1 spot on LMArena's Chatbot Arena Text leaderboard (Style Control On) through May 31, following its late-April release that propelled it to a leading 1504 Elo score—11 points ahead of Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at 1493. This adjustment mitigates stylistic biases in blind human evaluations, highlighting substantive capabilities in complex reasoning and agentic tasks where Anthropic excels. Google's 14% share reflects its close positioning and history of rapid iterations, while OpenAI and xAI trail due to recent models underperforming here despite April's competitive releases like GPT-5.x. Key catalysts ahead include potential frontier large language model drops before resolution.

markets.question: Will the St. Louis Cardinals win the 2026 World Series?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB World Series. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLB World Series per the rules of MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from MLB (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Los Angeles Dodgers lead trader consensus for the 2026 World Series after posting MLB's best starting rotation ERA at 2.79 through the first month, pacing for 110 wins with hot contributions from Andy Pages' MLB-leading hits and RBIs, solidifying their No. 1 power ranking. Atlanta Braves sit close behind at No. 2, fueled by Matt Olson's blazing early-season bat amid a strong NL East start, while New York Yankees hold No. 3 powered by Aaron Judge's 12 home runs tying the MLB lead and a 20-11 AL East record. Seattle Mariners, Chicago Cubs, San Diego Padres, and Detroit Tigers round out value plays with impressive early records and roster depth, but the wide-open field reflects inherent early-season volatility, division races, and injury uncertainties in a 162-game grind.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an elevated likelihood of EUR/USD testing 1.20 or higher in 2026 at 91% implied probability for that threshold, reflecting expectations of narrowing U.S.-eurozone policy divergence amid the Federal Reserve's federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% post-April 28-29 meeting, versus ECB deposit facility at 2.00% following its April 30 hold. The pair trades near 1.174 after a modest uptick, buoyed by hawkish ECB signals on surging 3.0% April flash CPI exceeding forecasts, despite Strait of Hormuz tensions elevating oil prices and pressuring eurozone growth. Key swings hinge on U.S. April CPI release May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC, where softer inflation could prompt Fed cuts and boost EUR/USD upside.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.5%-3.75% after the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, underscoring solid economic expansion, robust job gains with March unemployment at 4.3%, and persistent inflation as March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year. Trader consensus, reflected in CME FedWatch Tool probabilities and elevated two-year Treasury yields around 3.9%, prices prolonged steady rates through 2026 amid sticky price pressures and resilient labor markets, diminishing near-term cut expectations. Upcoming catalysts include April CPI data on May 12—due before the next FOMC on June 16-17—which could recalibrate market-implied rate paths toward 2027.

Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93% implied probability against direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, driven by Pyongyang's entrenched hostility and prioritization of military advancements over dialogue. North Korea's multiple short-range ballistic missile tests on April 19 underscored ongoing escalation, while Kim Jong Un's March formalization of South Korea as its "primary hostile state" remains the dominant policy framework, blocking bilateral engagement. Recent high-level visits from Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin on April 27 and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on April 10 highlight Pyongyang's deepening alliances with Moscow and Beijing, diverting focus from Seoul. South Korea's early April apology for a drone incursion elicited a mild North Korean response but no reciprocal overtures or scheduled negotiations, reinforcing structural barriers absent late-breaking diplomatic shifts.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Elon Bull Run Parlay, with "No" at an 88% implied probability, reflects the compounded unlikelihood of all three 2026 milestones aligning: Elon Musk reaching trillionaire status per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches to 100 km altitude. As of early May, Musk's net worth hovers around $800 billion—buoyed by Tesla's $573 million in sales to xAI and SpaceX but far from doubling amid volatile markets—while no new baby has been announced. Starship progress lags with just one qualifying flight (Flight 12 on April 7), despite Flight 13 slated for early May and FAA approvals for up to 44 launches annually; historical cadence and technical hurdles temper expectations for the required pace. Recent Tesla-xAI investments signal synergies, but traders see slim odds for the full parlay by year-end.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham holds a commanding lead in trader consensus at 86% implied probability for the South Carolina Republican Senate primary on June 9, driven by his substantial fundraising edge—over $20 million raised versus challenger Mark Lynch's $5.7 million self-funded campaign—and long-held incumbency advantages in the GOP stronghold. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors 15°C (28.5%) and 16°C (23.5%) as the lowest temperature in Tokyo on May 3, reflecting close alignment in recent Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) and global model ensembles like GFS and ECMWF, which project overnight lows in the mid-teens Celsius under mild high-pressure ridging over the Kanto region. These probabilities capture springtime climatology, where early May averages hover around 14–16°C due to moderating sea breezes from Tokyo Bay and urban heat island effects limiting radiational cooling, though variability arises from cloud cover persistence or subtle northerly flows in the latest 12–24-hour model runs showing ensemble spreads of 2–3°C. Lower odds for 14°C or below stem from low historical frequency below climatological norms absent cold fronts, while warmer outcomes face barriers from typical nighttime inversions; watch JMA's next 00Z forecast update for refinements ahead of resolution based on official Tokyo observations.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates Kimi Antonelli and George Russell dominate the early 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship standings after three Grands Prix, with Antonelli leading at 72 points ahead of Russell's 63 following Mercedes 1-2 finishes in Australia and China, plus Antonelli's Japanese GP victory from pole. The tight trader consensus—Russell at 39.5% implied probability over Antonelli's 32.6%—reflects their intra-team battle under new regulations favoring Mercedes' superior race pace and reliability, Russell's experience edging out the young Italian's flawless starts despite occasional position losses at lights-out. Leclerc trails at 49 points, underscoring Mercedes' constructors' lead and the duo's podium lockouts, while challengers like Verstappen face DNF risks and setup struggles keeping the title fight fiercely competitive through Miami's sprint weekend.

markets.question: Will Cleveland Browns win the 2026 AFC North?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC North division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the Ravens as AFC North favorites at 50.5% implied probability, reflecting their retention of the core from last season's 13-4 division title around elite QB Lamar Jackson, augmented by free-agent RB Derrick Henry to enhance the league's top rushing attack and a stout defense. Recent 2024 NFL Draft additions like CB Nate Wiggins via trade-up further solidify their secondary depth, maintaining edge over rivals. Bengals trail at 28.5% on a healthy Joe Burrow's return with WR Ja'Marr Chase, though OT losses loom. Browns' 12.5% hinges on top-ranked defense offsetting Deshaun Watson's injury history, while Steelers' 6.5% underscores QB uncertainty with Russell Wilson and Justin Fields amid O-line upgrades. No major injuries or trades have shifted sentiment post-draft.
